name: Clifftide — Cliff Gate
description: Massive timber-and-stone gates stand open between two squared-off guard towers, marking the landward entrance to Clifftide enclave. The construction is unmistakably Farborn — no scrollwork, no carved flourishes, just precisely fitted stone and ironbound oak built to withstand siege. Legion banners snap in the salt wind above, their device a broken chain on grey. Kin visitors often hesitate at this threshold, their kin-sense registering the faint wrongness of Breach Static from the Farborn guards who watch from the towers with disciplined, unreadable expressions.

name: Clifftide — Sea-Wall Path (East)
description: A broad stone path runs along the top of a thick sea-wall that protects Clifftide's lower buildings from storm surge. The wall is Farborn engineering at its most practical — rough-hewn granite blocks fitted without mortar, angled to deflect wave force rather than resist it head-on. Salt spray mists upward from the rocks below, and seabirds wheel overhead in raucous clouds. To the south, the enclave's buildings step down the cliff face in orderly terraces.

name: Clifftide — Sea-Wall Path (West)
description: The sea-wall path continues westward, offering a commanding view of the grey-green ocean stretching to the horizon. Wind-scoured flagstones underfoot bear the wear of three centuries of Farborn boots. A stone bench has been set into the wall's inner face, worn smooth by generations of sentries taking their rest between watches. The crash of waves against the cliff base below is a constant, rhythmic thunder that never quite fades from awareness.

name: Clifftide — Lookout Tower
description: A square stone tower rises from the western end of the sea-wall, its flat roof bristling with signal flags and a bronze fire-basket for night warnings. The tower is the highest point in Clifftide, and from its base the entire enclave is visible — terraced buildings stepping down the cliff, the stable yards to the south, the fishing dock far below. Farborn sentries maintain a round-the-clock watch here, scanning both sea and land with the ingrained vigilance of a people who remember what it means to be hunted.

name: Clifftide — Training Yard
description: A flat expanse of packed earth and crushed shell serves as Clifftide's primary training ground, surrounded by weapon racks and practice dummies made of salt-hardened timber. Farborn soldiers drill here daily regardless of weather, their movements precise and synchronized in a tradition passed down from the slave-legions that won their freedom at Dark Dawn. The yard bears scorch marks, gouges, and the particular scuffs of heavy shield work. Even off-duty Farborn move through this space with an unconscious martial awareness that Kin visitors find subtly unnerving.

name: Clifftide — Barracks Row (North)
description: A row of long, low stone buildings lines both sides of this narrow lane, their timber doors identically spaced and uniformly maintained. These are the barracks of Clifftide's standing garrison, each building housing a squad of eight in the old Legion fashion. The interiors are spartan — stacked bunks, weapon pegs, shared footlockers. Salt wind whistles between the buildings, carrying the smell of the sea and the clang of the training yard to the south.

name: Clifftide — Barracks Row (South)
description: The barracks lane continues between more identical stone buildings, though here a few personal touches have crept in over the centuries — a wind chime of shell and iron, a carved driftwood figure in a window niche, a small herb pot sheltered from the wind. These small rebellions against uniformity speak to the Farborn's gradual evolution from military unit to community. The lane opens eastward toward the heart of the enclave and westward toward the forge-shrine.

name: Clifftide — Gonmareck Ritler Forge-Shrine
description: Heat and the ring of hammer on anvil pour from this open-fronted stone building where forge and altar are one and the same. The shrine to Gonmareck Ritler, god of smiths and discipline, is the strongest religious bridge between Farborn and Kin — here the Farborn's reverence for craft and order finds its purest expression. A great iron anvil serves as both working surface and altar, its face inscribed with prayers in the angular Farborn script. The forge fire never goes out, tended in shifts by devotee-smiths who consider the work itself a form of worship.

name: Clifftide — Armory
description: Iron-banded doors open into a cool, dry chamber carved directly into the cliff face, its walls lined with weapon racks and armor stands maintained to exacting Legion standards. Every blade is oiled, every shield strap supple, every spearhead sharp — the Farborn have never forgotten the cost of unpreparedness. A senior armorer oversees the collection with the quiet authority of a librarian guarding irreplaceable texts. The armory connects to the forge-shrine above, ensuring a constant flow of repaired and newly forged equipment.

name: Clifftide — Commander's Hall
description: The largest building in Clifftide serves as both seat of governance and strategic command center for the entire Chainless Legion. Stone walls two feet thick surround a central hall where Commander Drath Steelborn holds council. A great table of salt-bleached oak dominates the room, its surface scored with map lines and campaign markers from three hundred years of coastal defense. The hall is austere but not cold — banners of the freed legions hang from the rafters, each one representing a unit that broke its chains at Dark Dawn.

name: Clifftide — Mess Hall
description: Long trestle tables and benches fill this broad, low-ceilinged hall where the garrison and much of the civilian population take their meals communally. The smell of salted fish stew, dense bread, and kelp broth hangs permanently in the air. Meals are served at fixed times in the old military fashion, though families now eat together rather than by squad. A great iron cauldron hangs over a central fire pit, large enough to feed the entire enclave in lean times.

name: Clifftide — Residential Quarter (North)
description: Stone cottages with heavy timber doors cluster along a narrow lane that follows the cliff's contour, each dwelling built to the same practical dimensions but slowly individualized over generations. Farborn families live here — children play in the lane with the same disciplined energy their parents bring to drill, organizing themselves into squads for their games. The Breach Static hum is strongest in these residential areas where many Farborn gather, a sensation like standing near a beehive that Kin visitors feel in their bones rather than their ears.

name: Clifftide — Residential Quarter (South)
description: The residential lane winds southward past more stone cottages, ending at a rough stone stairway that descends the cliff face toward the shore below. Laundry lines stretch between buildings, salt-stiffened clothing snapping like signal flags in the coastal wind. An elderly Farborn woman tends a small garden of wind-resistant herbs in a sheltered nook between two buildings, coaxing green life from the thin cliff-top soil with the same determination her ancestors used to forge a nation from nothing.

name: Clifftide — Cliff Path
description: A steep stone stairway carved into the cliff face zigzags down from the enclave above to the shore below, each step worn smooth by centuries of use. Iron chain handrails bolted into the rock provide stability against the wind that gusts unpredictably along the cliff face. The descent offers a vertiginous view of the waves smashing against the rocks far below, and the tiny fishing dock that clings to the sheltered cove at the cliff's base. Salt spray makes the steps treacherous in rough weather.

name: Clifftide — Shore Landing
description: At the base of the cliff path, a narrow strip of dark shingle beach opens onto a sheltered cove where the waves are calmer than along the open coast. Driftwood and kelp pile against the cliff base, and tide pools glimmer in rocky depressions. The air is thick with salt and the iodine smell of exposed seaweed. Above, the enclave perches on the cliff edge like a fortress, its stone foundations merging seamlessly with the natural rock.

name: Clifftide — Fishing Dock
description: A small but sturdy timber dock extends into the sheltered cove, its pilings driven deep into the rocky seabed and reinforced with iron bands against the relentless salt water. A handful of fishing boats are moored here — small, practical vessels built for the rough coastal waters. Nets hang drying on wooden frames, and baskets of the day's catch sit ready for hauling up the cliff path. The dock is functional rather than a true harbor, serving Clifftide's subsistence needs rather than any trade ambitions.

name: Clifftide — Market Square
description: A small, wind-swept square serves as Clifftide's market — practical rather than bustling, with stone-walled stalls that can be shuttered against storms. Goods here are necessities: rope, iron fittings, preserved food, leather, and the occasional luxury traded up from Saltrest Haven. Farborn merchants conduct business with the same brisk efficiency they bring to everything, and prices are fair by community consensus rather than haggling. A few Kin traders have begun appearing in recent decades, their discomfort with the Breach Static slowly overcome by reliable profit.

name: Clifftide — Supply Warehouse
description: A large stone building with reinforced doors serves as Clifftide's central supply depot, its interior organized with military precision into labeled sections for grain, salt, timber, iron stock, and preserved goods. The warehouse is kept cool and dry by clever ventilation shafts carved through the cliff rock. Inventory is maintained on slate boards hung by the entrance, updated daily in the angular Farborn script. Three centuries of careful supply management have made the Farborn experts at sustaining a community on a hostile coastline.

name: Clifftide — Stable Complex (Outer Yard)
description: A broad, walled yard opens south of the market square, its packed-earth floor scarred by heavy hoofprints and scattered with straw. This is the outer yard of Clifftide's famous Titanbreed horse operation — the massive warhorses bred by the Farborn from stock they brought through the Breach at Dark Dawn. The animals are enormous, standing half again as tall as common horses, with broad chests and thick legs bred for carrying armored riders. Their presence fills the yard with the smell of horse and hay.

name: Clifftide — Stable Complex (Inner Stables)
description: Massive stone-and-timber stalls line both sides of a covered stable building, each stall large enough to house the Titanbreed warhorses that are Clifftide's pride and most valuable asset. The horses watch visitors with an intelligence that seems almost uncanny, their dark eyes calm and assessing. Farborn grooms move between the stalls with quiet competence, speaking to the animals in low, steady voices. The breeding records, maintained on leather scrolls in the tack room, trace every Titanbreed bloodline back to the original seven mares that survived the crossing from the Farborn's home plane.

name: Saltrest Haven — Harbor Gate
description: A timber archway marks the entrance to Saltrest Haven, more welcoming than Clifftide's fortress gate but still solidly built in the Farborn fashion. The arch is decorated with carved fish and rope motifs — the closest thing to ornamentation the Farborn permit themselves. Beyond the gate, the enclave spreads down a gentle slope toward the natural harbor below. The smell of salt, drying fish, and woodsmoke drifts on the breeze, and the sounds of a working fishing community replace the military rhythms of Clifftide.

name: Saltrest Haven — Harbor Road
description: A broad dirt road slopes down toward the harbor, its surface packed hard by generations of foot traffic and handcarts. Buildings line both sides — timber-framed with stone foundations in the standard Farborn style, but here there is more variation, more personality. Window boxes hold salt-tolerant flowers, and doorways are painted in faded blues and greens. The atmosphere is noticeably more relaxed than Clifftide's military precision, though the underlying Farborn practicality remains in every well-maintained structure.

name: Saltrest Haven — Fish Market
description: Stone slabs and timber counters fill an open-air market where the morning's catch is displayed, sorted, and sold with practiced efficiency. Silvery fish of a dozen varieties lie on beds of crushed ice hauled from the storage caves, while dried and salt-cured fish hang in long rows from overhead racks. Farborn fishwives call out the day's prices in their distinctive clipped accent, and Halfling traders from the nearby trade post examine the goods with commercial interest. The smell is overwhelming — brine, fish oil, and the sharp tang of fresh-split ice.

name: Saltrest Haven — Harbormaster's Office
description: A squat stone building overlooking the harbor serves as the administrative heart of Saltrest Haven's fishing and trade operations. Charts of coastal waters, tide tables, and weather records cover every wall, maintained with the meticulous record-keeping the Farborn are known for. The harbormaster — a weathered Farborn woman with salt-grey hair — manages the harbor from a desk buried under ledgers and cargo manifests. Through the wide front window, the entire harbor is visible, from the fishing boats at their moorings to the salt flats beyond.

name: Saltrest Haven — Harbor Dock
description: A proper wooden dock extends into Saltrest Haven's natural harbor, longer and better maintained than Clifftide's small fishing pier. A dozen boats can moor here comfortably, from small dinghies to the broader-beamed fishing vessels that work the deeper waters offshore. Rope coils, crab pots, and stacked nets occupy every spare inch of dock space. The water here is calmer than the open coast, sheltered by a natural breakwater of tumbled boulders that the Farborn have reinforced with fitted stone over the centuries.

name: Saltrest Haven — Boat Yard
description: An open yard at the harbor's edge serves as Saltrest Haven's boat-building and repair facility, its ground littered with wood shavings, bent planks, and the tools of the shipwright's trade. Two boats sit in dry dock on heavy timber cradles — one being recaulked, another having a cracked strake replaced. The Farborn boat-builders work with the same methodical precision their ancestors brought to military engineering, producing vessels that are plain but seaworthy enough to survive the rough coastal waters year after year.

name: Saltrest Haven — Residential Lane (North)
description: A narrow lane winds between stone-and-timber cottages, their roofs shingled with overlapping slate tiles against the coastal rain. Life here moves at a gentler pace than in Clifftide — children play in the lane, an old man mends a fishing net on his doorstep, and the smell of cooking drifts from open windows. The Breach Static of the gathered Farborn population hums beneath perception, a subtle wrongness that visiting Kin describe as the feeling of a word forgotten on the tip of the tongue.

name: Saltrest Haven — Residential Lane (South)
description: The residential lane continues southward, curving gently toward the harbor. Here the cottages are older, some dating back to the first generation of Farborn settlers who built Saltrest Haven as a fishing camp before it grew into a proper community. Foundation stones bear carved dates in the angular Farborn calendar, marking the years since Dark Dawn. A communal well stands at a small intersection, its stone rim worn to a polish by three centuries of rope and bucket.

name: Saltrest Haven — Net-Mending Area
description: An open-sided shelter with a heavy timber roof provides a communal workspace where Saltrest Haven's fishing nets are repaired, maintained, and sometimes rebuilt from scratch. Nets in various states of repair hang from wooden frames and drape over trestles, and the ground is thick with discarded twine and broken cork floats. This is a social hub as much as a work space — fisherfolk gather here to mend nets and exchange news, their quick hands working knots while conversation flows freely in the Farborn dialect that still carries traces of their home plane's tongue.

name: Saltrest Haven — Watchtower
description: A timber watchtower rises above the rooftops of Saltrest Haven, less imposing than Clifftide's stone lookout but serving the same essential purpose. The tower offers a clear view of the harbor approach, the coastal road, and the salt flats to the west. A single sentry keeps watch during daylight hours, with a bell and signal fire ready for emergencies. The watchtower is the most overtly military structure in the otherwise civilian enclave, a reminder that even three centuries of peace have not erased the Farborn instinct for vigilance.

name: Saltrest Haven — Salt Flats Approach
description: The lane opens westward onto a broad, flat expanse where the tide retreats to reveal shallow pools that the Farborn have channeled and walled into an efficient salt-harvesting operation. Stone-lined evaporation pans stretch in neat rows, their white-crusted surfaces glittering in the sun. The air here is thick with brine, and the ground crunches underfoot with crystallized salt. This operation gives the enclave its name and its most reliable trade good — Farborn sea salt, prized for its clean flavor and consistent quality.

name: Saltrest Haven — Halfling Trade Post
description: A timber building slightly different in character from its Farborn neighbors houses the Halfling trade post — the first permanent Kin-operated business in any of the three enclaves. The ceiling is lower than standard Farborn construction, the doorway rounded rather than squared, and the interior smells of pipe tobacco and cinnamon rather than salt and iron. Halfling merchants from the river settlements were the first Kin to regularly trade with the Farborn, their mercantile pragmatism overcoming the unease of Breach Static long before other Kin peoples warmed to the Legion.

name: Saltrest Haven — The Broken Chain Tavern
description: The largest social gathering place in Saltrest Haven bears a name that encapsulates the Farborn identity — a heavy iron chain, broken at its center link, hangs above the door. Inside, the tavern is warm and dimly lit, with heavy timber furniture and a stone hearth that burns driftwood with blue-green flames. The Farborn brew a potent kelp ale that Kin visitors either love or despise, and salted fish of every variety accompanies every drink. Conversation here is louder and freer than anywhere else in the enclaves, and it is one of the few places where Farborn and visiting Kin regularly share a table.

name: Saltrest Haven — Salt Drying Racks
description: Row upon row of wooden drying racks stand in the open air beyond the salt flats, each one hung with split fish curing in the salt wind and sun. The smell is powerful — raw salt, drying fish, and the mineral tang of evaporating brine. Farborn workers move between the racks with quiet efficiency, turning fish, checking cures, and replacing any rack timbers weakened by the corrosive salt air. This operation produces the salt-cured fish that is Saltrest Haven's primary export, traded up and down the coast and increasingly into Kin markets.

name: Saltrest Haven — Community Hall
description: A large stone-and-timber hall serves as Saltrest Haven's gathering place for community decisions, celebrations, and the occasional solemn remembrance of Dark Dawn. The hall is plain inside — wooden benches arranged in concentric semicircles facing a raised stone platform where speakers address the community. Unlike Clifftide's military command structure, Saltrest Haven governs itself by community consensus, a tradition that has evolved over generations. The walls bear simple painted murals depicting the founding of the enclave and the fishing fleet's first great catch.

name: Saltrest Haven — Gonmareck Ritler Shrine
description: A small but well-maintained shrine to Gonmareck Ritler occupies a quiet corner of the enclave, its forge-altar smaller than Clifftide's but equally well-tended. Here the emphasis is less on martial smithing and more on the tools of daily labor — fishing hooks, boat nails, kitchen knives, and the iron fittings that hold the enclave's buildings together. The shrine's keeper is both priest and practical smith, and any Farborn or Kin may request a tool be blessed at the altar. The forge fire burns with a steady orange glow, and the ring of small hammer work carries softly through the surrounding lanes.

name: Saltrest Haven — Storage Caves (Entrance)
description: A natural cave mouth in the low cliff behind Saltrest Haven has been enlarged and reinforced by Farborn engineers to create a series of cool, dry storage chambers. The entrance is fitted with heavy timber doors and a drainage channel that keeps the interior free of standing water. Crates of preserved food, barrels of salt, coils of rope, and stacks of dried timber fill the antechamber, all organized with the standard Farborn obsession for orderly inventory. The cave extends deeper into the cliff, its further chambers used for longer-term storage.

name: Saltrest Haven — Storage Caves (Deep Chamber)
description: The deeper chambers of the storage caves maintain a constant cool temperature year-round, making them ideal for preserving perishable goods through the hot coastal summers. Barrels of kelp ale, wheels of the hard cheese traded from Halfling merchants, and sealed crocks of pickled vegetables line the rough stone walls. Farborn workers come and go with hand-lanterns, their footsteps echoing in the stone corridors. The air is cool and still, carrying the faint mineral smell of underground water seeping through limestone.

name: Saltrest Haven — Coastal Path (North)
description: A worn footpath leads north along the coast from Saltrest Haven's harbor gate, following the cliff edge above the churning sea. Wind-bent grass and hardy coastal shrubs line the path, and seabirds nest in the cliff face below, their cries mingling with the crash of waves. The path is well-traveled — Farborn from all three enclaves use these coastal trails to maintain the connections that bind their scattered communities together. Ahead to the north, the path joins the wider coastal road toward Clifftide.

name: Saltrest Haven — Coastal Path (South)
description: The coastal footpath leads south from Saltrest Haven's salt flats toward the more isolated Redcurrent Strand, winding along the cliff tops above rocky coves and narrow beaches. The vegetation thins as the path moves away from the enclave, leaving exposed stretches where the wind hits with full force. Marker stones set by the Farborn ensure travelers can follow the route even in poor visibility, each one carved with a simple directional arrow and the distance to the nearest enclave in the angular Farborn script.

name: Redcurrent Strand — Beach Approach
description: The coastal path descends to a wide, dark-sand beach where the waves roll in stained with the distinctive red-brown tint that gives this place its name. The red current — caused by mineral-rich runoff from iron deposits in the coastal cliffs — colors the water in bands of rust and amber that shift with the tide. The beach stretches south toward the enclave's simple timber gate, and the wind carries the smell of salt, iron, and the faint smoke of the community fire pit. The atmosphere here is quieter, more contemplative than the other enclaves.

name: Redcurrent Strand — Strand Gate
description: A simple timber gate marks the boundary of Redcurrent Strand, the smallest and most isolated of the three Chainless Legion enclaves. Where Clifftide has fortress walls and Saltrest Haven has a welcoming arch, Redcurrent's gate is modest — two weathered posts and a crossbeam, more symbolic boundary than defensive structure. The enclave beyond is a loose collection of huts and communal buildings scattered along the strand, connected by sand paths rather than stone roads. The red-tinted surf crashes softly on the beach to the west.

name: Redcurrent Strand — Community Fire Pit
description: A broad stone-lined fire pit serves as the social heart of Redcurrent Strand, its embers kept glowing throughout the day and built into a proper blaze each evening. Driftwood benches circle the pit, worn smooth by years of use, and the sand around it is packed hard by countless gathered feet. Here the Farborn of Redcurrent share their meals, their stories, and their ongoing effort to understand what they are and where they came from. The fire pit faces the western ocean, and on clear evenings the community gathers to watch the sun sink into the red-stained waves.

name: Redcurrent Strand — Historian's Hall
description: A long, low building of stone and timber houses the offices and meeting space of Redcurrent Strand's historians — the scholars who have dedicated themselves to understanding the Farborn's origins and their passage through the Breach. The walls are covered with charts, timelines, and speculative maps of the plane the Farborn came from, reconstructed from fragmentary memories and the few physical records that survived Dark Dawn. The historians work with methodical Farborn patience, cross-referencing oral accounts with physical evidence, slowly assembling a picture of a lost world.

name: Redcurrent Strand — Library of Origins
description: The most precious space in all three enclaves is this modest stone chamber where the Library of Origins is housed — the collected pre-Dark Dawn records of the Farborn people. Leather scroll cases and bound volumes of pressed bark-paper fill shelves carved into the walls, each one preserved with obsessive care against salt, damp, and time. The collection is small by any library's standards, but to the Farborn it represents their entire surviving connection to the world they left behind. A keeper tends the collection full-time, controlling temperature and humidity with the dedication of a shrine-priest guarding sacred relics.

name: Redcurrent Strand — Central Path
description: A sand-and-gravel path winds through the center of the enclave, connecting the communal buildings in a rough line parallel to the shore. Dune grass grows in clumps along the path's edges, and salt-bleached driftwood has been used to mark the way where sand threatens to obscure it. The enclave's buildings are spread more loosely here than in the other settlements, giving Redcurrent Strand an open, almost rural quality. The constant sound of the red-tinted surf provides a backdrop to every conversation and every silence.

name: Redcurrent Strand — Meditation Circle
description: A ring of wave-smoothed stones set into the sand marks the meditation circle where Redcurrent's contemplative community practices the stillness exercises that have become central to Farborn spiritual life. Unlike the active forge-worship of Gonmareck Ritler, this practice seeks understanding through silence and inward focus, exploring the strange gap between the Farborn and the world's kin-sense. The circle faces the western ocean, and practitioners often meditate at dawn and dusk when the red current is most visible. Kin visitors who enter the circle sometimes report that the Breach Static feels different here — less wrong, more simply other.

name: Redcurrent Strand — Herb Garden
description: A sheltered hollow between two low dunes has been cultivated into a surprisingly productive herb garden, protected from the worst of the coastal wind by woven grass screens and driftwood fences. Salt-tolerant herbs grow in neat rows — samphire, sea lavender, shore mustard, and several species unknown to Kin herbalists that the Farborn cultivated from seeds brought through the Breach. The garden serves both culinary and medicinal purposes, and the Farborn gardener who tends it is one of the enclave's most respected members, bridging the gap between Farborn plant knowledge and the native flora of this world.

name: Redcurrent Strand — Residential Huts (West)
description: Sturdy huts built from timber, stone, and woven grass stand in a loose cluster along the upper beach, their walls thick against the coastal storms but their construction simpler than the military-standard buildings of Clifftide. Each hut houses a family or a small group of scholars, and the spaces between them serve as shared yards where daily tasks are performed in the open air. Drying racks for herbs, woodworking benches, and rain-collection barrels occupy the common ground. The Farborn here live simply, their focus turned inward toward scholarship and contemplation rather than outward toward defense or trade.

name: Redcurrent Strand — Residential Huts (South)
description: The southernmost residential huts of Redcurrent Strand sit at the edge of the enclave where the maintained paths give way to wild dune grass and rocky shore. These dwellings belong to the enclave's most reclusive members — scholars and contemplatives who prefer solitude for their work. The huts are well-built but deliberately spare, each one containing little more than a sleeping platform, a writing desk, and a shelf of personal texts. The sound of waves is loudest here, and on stormy nights the spray reaches the doorsteps.

name: Redcurrent Strand — Shore Path
description: A winding path follows the shoreline south of the enclave, tracing the edge of the red-tinted surf line where mineral-stained water meets dark sand. Tide pools dot the rocky stretches between sand, each one a miniature world of anemones, crabs, and small fish adapted to the iron-rich water. The path is used by foragers collecting shellfish and seaweed, and by scholars who walk here to think. Offshore, the red current is visible as a broad band of rust-colored water flowing southward, fed by the mineral springs hidden in the cliffs.

name: Redcurrent Strand — Tidal Pools
description: A broad shelf of exposed rock extends into the surf here, its surface carved into basins and channels by centuries of tidal action. The pools are stained red-brown by the mineral current, giving the trapped water a strange, blood-like appearance that belies the delicate life within — crimson anemones, rust-colored crabs, and tiny fish with scales that shimmer copper in the sunlight. Farborn naturalists have catalogued every species in these pools, finding several that exist nowhere else along the coast, adapted specifically to the iron-rich environment.

name: Redcurrent Strand — Small Dock
description: A modest timber dock extends from the rocky shore into the calmer water behind a natural stone breakwater, its planks stained rust-red by years of exposure to the mineral-laden surf. A handful of small boats are tied here — rowing dinghies and shallow-draft fishing craft unsuited to open water but perfect for working the sheltered coves nearby. The dock is functional but aging, repaired with whatever timber driftwood and trade can provide. Nets and crab pots hang from the dock pilings, and a salt-crusted rope ladder descends to the waterline.

name: Redcurrent Strand — Keeper's Cottage
description: A solitary stone cottage stands on a low rise overlooking the dock and the red-tinted waters beyond, home to the Keeper of the Strand — the elected leader of Redcurrent's small community. The cottage is larger than the residential huts but equally plain, its only distinction a carved stone lintel depicting the red current itself as a flowing ribbon of intertwined lines. The Keeper serves as mediator, record-keeper, and representative to the other enclaves, a role that requires the quiet authority the contemplative community values over martial strength.

name: Redcurrent Strand — Watchtower
description: A weathered timber watchtower stands on the highest dune at the western edge of Redcurrent Strand, offering a wide view of the coastline in both directions. The tower is the enclave's only concession to military necessity, maintained more out of Farborn habit than genuine expectation of threat. A bronze bell hangs at the top for emergencies, and a small covered platform provides shelter for the single watchkeeper who takes a daily shift. From here, the red current is visible far out to sea, a broad ribbon of rust-colored water that traces the coast southward until it fades into the grey-green ocean.

name: Redcurrent Strand — Coastal Trail (South)
description: A rough trail leads south from the watchtower along increasingly wild coastline, where the enclave's maintained paths give way to bare rock and wind-flattened dune grass. The trail connects Redcurrent Strand to the southern stretches of the coastal wilderness road, used occasionally by messengers and patrols but otherwise left to the seabirds and the wind. The iron-rich cliffs rise to the east, their faces streaked red and orange where mineral springs seep through the rock, feeding the red current that defines this stretch of coast.

name: Redcurrent Strand — Shrine Alcove
description: A natural alcove in the coastal cliff has been shaped into a small shrine to Gonmareck Ritler, the stone walls carved with the god's hammer-and-anvil symbol and the Farborn broken-chain motif. A tiny forge — barely more than a brazier and a hand bellows — sits against the back wall, used for small devotional smithing rather than practical work. The shrine is intimate and quiet, sheltered from the wind, and the sound of waves echoing in the alcove creates a rhythmic backdrop to prayer. Iron offerings — nails, fishhooks, small blades — are piled in a stone basin before the forge.

name: Redcurrent Strand — Dune Overlook
description: A high dune at the eastern edge of the enclave offers a panoramic view of Redcurrent Strand spread out below — the loose scatter of huts, the fire pit's thin smoke trail, the dark beach with its rust-colored surf line, and the dock's tiny silhouette against the grey water. Inland, the coastal grassland stretches east toward the distant line of trees marking the approach to Twin Rivers territory. Wind-bent grass whispers around the dune's crest, and the isolation of this smallest enclave is palpable from this vantage point — a community of scholars and thinkers perched at the edge of the world.

name: Redcurrent Strand — Seaweed Drying Beds
description: Flat stones arranged on the upper beach serve as drying beds for harvested seaweed, spread in thin layers to cure in the salt wind and sun. The Farborn use dried seaweed as food, fertilizer, and even building insulation, wasting nothing the sea provides. Several varieties are laid out in neat sections — broad brown kelp, fine green sea lettuce, and the dark purple dulse that grows only in the mineral-rich waters of the red current. The smell is intensely marine, a concentrated essence of the ocean that clings to clothing and hair long after leaving.

name: Redcurrent Strand — Cliff Base Springs
description: Where the coastal cliffs meet the beach, mineral-rich springs seep from cracks in the iron-veined rock, their water tinted rust-red as it trickles down to join the surf. These springs are the source of the red current that gives the enclave its name, and the Farborn have built small stone channels to collect the mineral water for various uses. The iron content makes it unsuitable for drinking, but it is prized for dyeing cloth, treating leather, and — the historians believe — may have properties connected to the Breach itself, though this remains speculation. The cliff face here is streaked in vivid bands of red, orange, and ochre.

name: Coastal Wilderness — Clifftide East Road
description: A well-maintained dirt road leads east from Clifftide's gate, cutting through wind-swept coastal grassland toward the interior. The road is wide enough for a cart and shows regular use — wheel ruts, hoofprints of Titanbreed horses, and the bootmarks of Farborn patrols. To the west, Clifftide's stone towers are visible on the cliff edge. To the east, the grassland rises gradually toward the forested hills that mark the boundary of Twin Rivers territory. Marker stones line the road at regular intervals, each one carved with distance markers in the angular Farborn script.

name: Coastal Wilderness — Eastern Grassland
description: The road continues east through open grassland, the coastal wind still strong but the salt smell fading as the distance from the sea increases. Tall grass ripples in waves on either side of the road, dotted with wildflowers in season and the occasional wind-stunted shrub. Ground-nesting birds burst from the grass at the approach of travelers, and rabbits bolt for their burrows. The land is unclaimed — neither Farborn nor Kin territory, a neutral buffer that both sides have tacitly agreed to leave empty.

name: Coastal Wilderness — Hill Crest
description: The road crests a low hill that offers a view in every direction — the grey ocean and Clifftide's towers to the west, rolling grassland to north and south, and the dark green line of forest to the east marking the edge of Twin Rivers territory. A cairn of stacked stones marks the hilltop, built by Farborn patrols as a waypoint and landmark. The wind is fierce and unbroken here, flattening the grass and making the cairn's small signal pennant snap taut. On clear days, all three enclaves are visible from this point as distant clusters of stone and timber on the coastal cliffs.

name: Coastal Wilderness — Forest Approach
description: The grassland gives way to scattered trees as the road approaches the forest marking Twin Rivers territory. The trees are wind-shaped on the western side, their branches reaching permanently eastward like frozen flags, but they grow taller and straighter as the forest thickens ahead. Birdsong replaces the cry of seabirds, and the ground underfoot changes from sandy coastal soil to darker, richer forest earth. The road narrows here, less frequently traveled on this side where Farborn patrols turn back and Kin woodsmen rarely venture west.

name: Coastal Wilderness — Coastal Road (North of Clifftide)
description: The coastal road runs along the cliff tops south of the Clifftide east road junction, a packed-earth track following the contour of the land above the crashing sea. To the north, the road leads back toward Clifftide's approach. To the south, it continues along the rugged coastline toward the Saltrest crossroads. Wind-bent grass and salt-tolerant scrub line both sides of the road, and the occasional Farborn patrol marker identifies this as a maintained route. Seabirds ride the updrafts from the cliffs, hanging motionless in the wind before diving toward the waves far below.

name: Coastal Wilderness — Coastal Road (Clifftide-Saltrest Mid)
description: The coastal road winds along the cliff edge between Clifftide and Saltrest Haven, its packed-earth surface showing the wear of regular foot traffic between the enclaves. Rocky outcrops force the road inland in places, creating brief sheltered stretches where the wind drops and the sound of waves becomes a distant murmur. Wildflowers grow in these sheltered pockets — sea thrift, cliff campion, and the yellow-flowering samphire that the Farborn harvest for cooking. The road is well-marked with carved stone waypoints.

name: Coastal Wilderness — Crossroads at Saltrest
description: The coastal road meets the eastern approach to Saltrest Haven at a marked crossroads, its center post carved with directional arrows and distances in the Farborn script — Clifftide to the north, Redcurrent Strand to the south, and the interior to the east. This is the busiest intersection in the Chainless Legion territory, where travelers between the three enclaves and the occasional Kin trader converge. A stone bench and a rain shelter offer respite to travelers, and a carved wooden board displays community notices from all three enclaves.

name: Coastal Wilderness — Coastal Road (South of Saltrest)
description: The coastal road descends southward from the Saltrest coastal path toward Redcurrent Strand, the terrain growing rougher and more exposed as the cliffs become higher and the wind more fierce. The road here is narrower and less maintained than the northern stretches, reflecting Redcurrent's smaller population and more isolated character. Iron-stained rock begins to appear in the cliff faces, streaked with rust-red mineral deposits that hint at the red current's source below. The vegetation is sparse — wind-flattened grass and lichen-covered rock.

name: Coastal Wilderness — Coastal Road (North of Redcurrent)
description: The coastal road continues south through increasingly rugged terrain, the iron-stained cliffs growing taller and more dramatic with each mile. Patches of rust-red mineral stain appear on the road itself where groundwater seeps up through cracks in the underlying rock. The air carries a faint metallic tang that grows stronger to the south. A Farborn waypoint marker here gives distances to all three enclaves, the angular script freshly recarved by a recent patrol.

name: Coastal Wilderness — Redcurrent Approach
description: The coastal road reaches the northern approach to Redcurrent Strand, where the iron-rich cliffs give the landscape a distinctive ruddy hue. The road descends toward the dark sand beach visible below, and the surf is visibly tinted with the rust-colored mineral runoff that names the enclave. A simple stone marker carved with a wave-and-chain motif indicates the boundary of Redcurrent territory. The enclave itself is visible ahead — a modest scatter of buildings on the strand, less imposing than either Clifftide or Saltrest Haven but possessed of a quiet dignity.

name: Coastal Wilderness — Southern Coastal Path
description: South of Redcurrent Strand, the coastal road dwindles to a rough path along the cliff tops, maintained only by occasional Farborn patrols. The iron-stained cliffs continue here, their faces carved by wind and waves into dramatic overhangs and sea caves. The path is narrow and exposed, buffeted by wind from the open ocean to the west. Seabird colonies nest in the cliff faces, filling the air with raucous calls and the sharp smell of guano. This is the wildest stretch of the Chainless Legion coastline, beautiful in its austerity.

name: Coastal Wilderness — Southern Cliffs
description: The coastal path reaches its southern extremity at a high, wind-battered promontory where the cliffs plunge vertically into churning grey-green water. This is the southernmost point of Chainless Legion territory, marked by a single standing stone carved with the broken-chain device and the date of Dark Dawn in the Farborn calendar. Beyond this point, the coast curves eastward and the land is unclaimed wilderness. The view is stark and magnificent — nothing but ocean to the south and west, and the long line of the iron-streaked coast stretching north toward the three enclaves.

name: Coastal Wilderness — Rocky Shoreline Path (South)
description: A rough path follows the rocky shoreline between the main coastal road sections, picking its way over tide-smoothed boulders and through gaps in the sea-carved rock. The path is usable only at low tide in some stretches, when the receding water reveals a narrow strip of dark gravel between the cliff base and the surf. Tide pools glimmer in every rocky depression, and the air is thick with the smell of exposed seaweed and brine. Farborn fisherfolk use this path to reach productive shellfish beds inaccessible from the cliff-top road above.

name: Coastal Wilderness — Rocky Shoreline Path (North)
description: The rocky shoreline path continues north along the base of the coastal cliffs, a treacherous but scenic route that offers a different perspective on the Chainless Legion coastline. The cliffs tower overhead, their faces streaked with salt stains and patches of yellow lichen, and the crash of waves against rock is deafening in the narrower passages. Caves and overhangs punctuate the cliff base, some large enough to shelter in during a sudden squall. The path eventually climbs back up to the cliff-top road near Clifftide's approach.

name: Coastal Wilderness — Clifftop Overlook
description: A flat-topped rock formation at the cliff's edge creates a natural overlook where the entire western coastline of the Chainless Legion territory is visible. On clear days, all three enclaves can be seen from here — Clifftide's towers to the north, Saltrest Haven's harbor in the middle distance, and Redcurrent Strand's modest scatter of buildings far to the south. The wind is relentless at this exposed point, carrying the combined sounds of waves, seabirds, and the distant ring of Clifftide's forge. Farborn scouts use this point for long-range coastal observation.

name: Coastal Wilderness — Wind-Swept Grassland (North)
description: Open grassland stretches across a gently undulating coastal plain north of the Clifftide road, the grass bent permanently eastward by the prevailing ocean wind. The ground is sandy and well-drained, supporting only the hardiest vegetation — salt grass, sea holly, and low-growing thyme that releases its scent when crushed underfoot. Rabbits are abundant here, and the occasional fox hunts among the grass tussocks. The grassland serves as common grazing for Clifftide's Titanbreed horses when they need open ground to run.

name: Coastal Wilderness — Inland Grassland (Saltrest East)
description: The grassland east of the Saltrest crossroads rises in gentle swells, the terrain shifting from coastal flatland to low rolling hills. A freshwater spring bubbles up from a rocky depression here, its water clean and cold, one of the few reliable water sources between the enclaves and the interior. The spring has been ringed with fitted stones in the Farborn fashion, functional and without embellishment. Rings of darker grass mark the sites of old campfires where Farborn patrols and messengers have rested for centuries.

name: Coastal Wilderness — Seabird Colony
description: A section of cliff top has been informally declared off-limits to regular traffic due to a massive seabird nesting colony that occupies every ledge, crack, and flat surface on the cliff face below. Thousands of birds wheel overhead in a cacophonous cloud of white and grey, their cries drowning out even the crash of waves. The ground near the cliff edge is thick with guano and scattered feathers, and the smell is eye-wateringly pungent. The Farborn harvest guano from the colony's edges as fertilizer for their gardens, but the birds themselves are left undisturbed — a rare instance of Farborn sentiment overriding practicality.

name: Coastal Wilderness — Abandoned Signal Post
description: The remains of an old Farborn signal post stand on a low hill between the coastal road and the interior, its timber frame weathered to silver-grey and its fire-basket rusted through. The post was part of an early warning network established in the first decades after Dark Dawn, when the Farborn still feared pursuit from the forces that had enslaved them. As centuries passed and no threat materialized from across the Breach, the outer signal posts were abandoned, though the main watchtowers in each enclave remain manned. The post is now a landmark and occasional shelter for travelers caught in coastal storms.

name: Coastal Wilderness — Eastern Road to Twin Rivers
description: The eastern road from the Chainless Legion territory meets the edge of the forest that marks the western boundary of Twin Rivers, transitioning from open coastal grassland to the shade of broadleaf trees. A carved stone boundary marker stands at the roadside, one face bearing the Farborn broken-chain device and the other left blank — a space that Kin authorities have never claimed, leaving the boundary formally unmarked from their side. The road is narrow here, little more than a cart track through the trees, but it represents the most direct overland connection between the Farborn enclaves and Kin civilization.
